description:
Advances: mutagen.mutators

Discovered during S7 (mutagen-wrd.9) end-to-end test driver iteration 3.

## Problem

`lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal.ex` declares `match?(0) -> true`, `match?(true) -> true`, etc. against the BARE value. `lib/mutagen_ex/ast_cache.ex:111` parses source with `Code.string_to_quoted(..., token_metadata: true, columns: true)`. With `token_metadata: true`, atomic literals are wrapped: the source `0` becomes `{:__block__, [token: "0", line: L, column: C], [0]}`, not the bare `0`.

The `MutagenEx.MutationEnumerator` walks the AST via `Macro.prewalk/3` and calls `mutator.match?(node)` on each node. For the wrapper `{:__block__, _, [0]}`, `Literal.match?` returns `false` (no clause matches the tuple shape). Prewalk then recurses into the children and visits the bare `0` — `match?` succeeds, but `node_line(0)` returns `nil` (literals are not 3-tuples; `node_line/1` only extracts meta from `{form, meta, args}` 3-tuples). Per `enumerator:224`, `is_nil(line) -> acc` filters the node out without producing a site or a skip.

Net effect: the `literal` mutator NEVER fires against any source parsed with `token_metadata: true`.

## Reproduction

Run `mix mutagen --scope test/fixtures/lane_project/lib/lane_fixture/decisions.ex --tests test/fixtures/lane_project/test/lane_fixture/decisions_test.exs` from the lane project's directory. The output `mutation.results` contains entries for `compare`, `boolean`, `case_drop`, and `else_removal` but NONE for `literal`, despite decisions.ex containing several `0`, `1`, `true`, `false` literals in covered code.

The lane-fixture S7 e2e test Scenario 2 originally asserted `Enum.any?` on compare/boolean/literal, which silently masked this gap. Iteration 3 tightened the assertion to `Enum.all?`, surfacing the bug — Scenario 2 currently asserts `literal` appears in results/skipped/compile_errors and would fail without a relaxation. The relaxation lives in `test/mutagen_ex/end_to_end_test.exs` Scenario 2 and points at this ticket.

## Resolution

`lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal.ex` needs `match?` clauses that destructure the `__block__` wrapper:

    def match?({:__block__, _meta, [true]}), do: true
    def match?({:__block__, _meta, [false]}), do: true
    def match?({:__block__, _meta, [0]}), do: true
    def match?({:__block__, _meta, [1]}), do: true
    def match?({:__block__, _meta, [-1]}), do: true
    def match?(_), do: false

And `mutate/1` needs to rebuild the wrapper preserving meta. This is a mechanical change inside `lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal.ex` plus an end-to-end test verifying the `__block__` shape lands on the right line in `results`.

## Related

- mutagen-wrd.9 (S7 e2e suite, where this was surfaced)
- mutagen-wrd.5 (mutator catalog + validate predicates; the unit tests there pass against the bare value but don't cover the AST-walked path)
- mutagen-wrd.6 (mutation enumerator; the line-filter logic at `enumerator:224` is correct given the literal mutator's current match? shape)

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: eb14f76b-662e-400e-ba6b-22e2f3772aea
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/eb14f76b-662e-400e-ba6b-22e2f3772aea.jsonl

Commit: 805e05df9872c9efcd0966ad44db44a22a2b03e7
Stage worktree: /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-literal-mutator-block-wrapper
Stage branch: mutagen-wrd.15/literal-mutator-block-wrapper (off epic/mutagen-wrd @ ca1c2b6)

Files touched:
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal.ex
- test/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal_test.exs
- test/mutagen_ex/mutators_property_test.exs
- test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs

Tests added:
- MutagenEx.Mutators.LiteralTest "match?/1 — __block__-wrapped values" (3 cases)
- MutagenEx.Mutators.LiteralTest "mutate/1 — __block__-wrapped values" (4 cases incl. token-strip semantics)
- MutagenEx.Mutators.LiteralTest "validate/1" — 2 new cases for wrapped values
- MutagenEx.Mutators.LiteralTest "AST round-trip (mutagen.mutators.r6)" — 2 cases (integer + boolean)
- MutagenEx.MutatorsPropertyTest "Literal (`__block__`-wrapped booleans)" involutive r5
- MutagenEx.MutatorsPropertyTest "Literal (`__block__`-wrapped 0/1)" involutive r5
- MutagenEx.MutatorsPropertyTest "Literal (`__block__`-wrapped, with `:token`)" token-strip + Macro.to_string
- MutagenEx.MutationEnumeratorTest "literal `__block__` wrapper end-to-end" (3 cases: bool site lands at wrapper line, int site lands at wrapper line, uncovered wrapper filtered before validate)

Verification stubs flipped: none new — the existing mutators.v1/v2/v3/v4 stubs were already `execute: true` and continue to pass. The new e2e enumerator test runs under the default mix test suite (no @tag :e2e_slow); the project verification command `mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test` passes (1 doctest, 322 tests, 0 failures, 13 excluded).

Deferred must requirements:
- decisions.ex literal floor (e2e Scenario 2a) → mutagen-wrd.16. The ticket's `__block__`-wrapper resolution is in place and verified end-to-end with a hand-built wrapped literal. But Elixir 1.19.5's parser does NOT wrap the literals in `test/fixtures/lane_project/lib/lane_fixture/decisions.ex` (`0` in `n > 0`, `1` in `do: 1`, etc.) — those appear as bare children of operator / clause-head tuples and carry no line metadata. The enumerator's `node_line/1 → nil → is_nil(line) → acc` filter at mutation_enumerator.ex:224 still drops them. Reaching the decisions.ex floor requires an enumerator-level parent-line-inheritance pass; filed as mutagen-wrd.16 (DISCOVERY: Literal mutator floor on decisions.ex needs enumerator parent-line-inheritance). Scenario 2a's `@tag :skip` therefore remains in place.

Summary: Added `__block__`-wrapper `match?/1`, `mutate/1`, and `validate/1` clauses to `lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal.ex` so a literal parsed with `token_metadata: true` and surfaced as `{:__block__, meta, [value]}` is matched, mutated (preserving positional meta so the enumerator attributes the swap to the wrapper's line), and validated. `mutate/1` strips the stale `:token` metadata so `Macro.to_string/1` renders the swapped value rather than reproducing the original source token (preserves `mutagen.mutators.r6`'s AST→source round trip). End-to-end coverage in `mutation_enumerator_test.exs` "literal `__block__` wrapper end-to-end" demonstrates a hand-built wrapped literal produces a site at the wrapper's line with stable site_id and meta-preserving `mutated_ast`.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: 9f7ff990-d3b6-45b5-b4a6-16900c792f95
Log: ~/.claude/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex-literal-mutator-block-wrapper/9f7ff990-d3b6-45b5-b4a6-16900c792f95.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 4
  - lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal.ex
  - test/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal_test.exs
  - test/mutagen_ex/mutators_property_test.exs
  - test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0 — ticket does not specify explicit Allowed-touches globs; the Resolution names lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal.ex plus an "end-to-end test verifying the `__block__` shape lands on the right line in `results`." All four touched files are within that implicit scope (the literal mutator + tests that exercise it via the mutators/property/enumerator test surfaces).
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 — ticket does not enumerate Out-of-Scope files. No production code outside lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal.ex was modified; mix.exs / mix.lock untouched (no new deps), end_to_end_test.exs untouched (Scenario 2a relaxation deferred to mutagen-wrd.16 — consistent).

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 4, passed: 4
  - mutators.v1 (`mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutators_test.exs`): 15 tests, 0 failures
  - mutators.v2 (`mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutators/id_stability_test.exs`): 4 tests, 0 failures
  - mutators.v3 (`mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutators_property_test.exs`): 11 tests, 0 failures
  - mutators.v4 (`mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutators/ --only validate`): 26 tests, 0 failures (77 excluded)
- Requirements coverage (one line per must requirement):
  - mutagen.mutators.r1 (match?/mutate/validate contract): covered by test/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal_test.exs:28 ("matches `{:__block__, meta, [literal]}` for each supported literal"), :71 ("preserves positional meta…"), :128 (":ok for `__block__`-wrapped booleans and integers"), AND end-to-end in test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs:519 ("boolean __block__ wrapper produces a literal site at the wrapper's line") which exercises the full match→mutate→validate→Site pipeline. Would fail if the new wrapped-`match?` clause regressed to `false` (enumerator test would assert `length(literal_sites) >= 1` and trip), if `mutate/1` returned a non-3-tuple shape (enumerator test asserts `{:__block__, meta, [false]} = literal_site.mutated_ast`), or if validate-on-wrapped returned `{:skip, _}` (literal_test.exs:128 asserts == :ok).
  - mutagen.mutators.r2 (`validate/1` skip reasons): covered by test/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal_test.exs:138 (":skip on non-literal") and :143 (":skip on `__block__` wrappers over non-literal values"). Would fail if `validate({:__block__, meta, [:atom]})` returned `:ok` instead of `{:skip, :structurally_invalid}` — directly tested.
  - mutagen.mutators.r3 (site ID shape `{file}:{ast_hash}:{mutator}`): covered by test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs:537 — asserts `literal_site.id == "#{file}:#{Mutators.ast_hash(injected_value)}:literal"`. Would fail if the mutator name changed, if the hash were computed over un-normalized AST, or if the file segment were dropped.
  - mutagen.mutators.r4 (IDs stable across `mix format`): covered transitively by the pre-existing test/mutagen_ex/mutators/id_stability_test.exs:69 golden over `arith`. For the literal-wrapper specifically, stability follows by construction: `MutagenEx.Mutators.normalize/1` strips only `:line/:column/:end_line/:end_column` (verified at lib/mutagen_ex/mutators.ex:143), and `mix format` only perturbs those positional keys. Would fail if normalize started preserving positional metadata, or if `mutate/1` injected positional keys that survived normalize. The wrapped form's `:token` is preserved by design (and by `:token` being part of AST content, two different source tokens hash differently — that's intended; site identity is per-occurrence in source).
  - mutagen.mutators.r5 (involutive swaps): covered by test/mutagen_ex/mutators_property_test.exs:91 ("Literal (`__block__`-wrapped booleans)") and :107 ("Literal (`__block__`-wrapped 0/1)") plus literal_test.exs:100 and :109. Would fail if `mutate(mutate(node)) != node` for any of {true, false, 0, 1} wrapped form on token-less meta. (-1 is asymmetric per the catalog: `-1 → 1` and `mutate(1) == 0`, so involution doesn't apply — consistent with the bare-value tests.)
  - mutagen.mutators.r6 (`Macro.to_string` round-trip after :ok validate): covered by test/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal_test.exs:151 (integer round-trip) and :172 (boolean round-trip) plus test/mutagen_ex/mutators_property_test.exs:117 (explicit token-strip rendering assertion). The integer case is the load-bearing one: it asserts `Macro.to_string(mutate(original_with_token)) == "1"` and that the reparsed AST equals `1`. Would fail if `mutate/1` left `:token` on the swapped wrapper (Macro.to_string would reproduce the original token "0" and the round-trip would land back on 0, not 1).
  - mutagen.mutators.r7 (catalog closed at 10 mutators): no change required; literal.ex still implements the same Mutators behaviour, no plugin interface added.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. The change is mechanical inside lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal.ex (per the ticket's "This is a mechanical change inside `lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal.ex`"). No new external deps. Implementer correctly deferred the decisions.ex floor (bare-literal parent-line-inheritance) to mutagen-wrd.16 because that fix lives in lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex, not literal.ex — verified that node_line/1 (mutation_enumerator.ex:295-296) still returns nil for bare `0`, so the deferral is structurally correct.
- Project verification: pass (`mix compile --warnings-as-errors` clean; `mix test`: 1 doctest, 322 tests, 0 failures, 13 excluded).
- Audit-expanded scope (per step 11), if any: none — no prior AUDIT: BOUNCE comments on this ticket.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- The .16 deferral is a legitimate scope partition. The implementer correctly distinguished the wrapped-shape contract (in scope here, covered) from the bare-literal floor on decisions.ex (which requires enumerator changes in a different file, .16). Spot-checked: node_line/1 at mutation_enumerator.ex:295 only reads meta from 3-tuples; bare `0` returns nil; r2's `is_nil(line) -> acc` filter at :224 drops the site. The wrapped-literal end-to-end test (mutation_enumerator_test.exs:519) demonstrates the ticket's contract works when the parser DOES produce the wrapper.
- The bare-value `match?` clauses are retained (literal.ex:60-64). They are unreachable end-to-end given the enumerator's nil-line filter today, but they document the contract for any future caller and keep the existing bare-value unit tests honest. Not a defect.
- Site IDs for bare-vs-wrapped intentionally differ (`:token` is preserved by normalize because it's part of the AST's semantic content). The implementer's moduledoc at literal.ex:45-50 calls this out. Consistent with mutagen.decision.content_addressed_ids which only strips positional keys.
- One untracked path exists in the worktree (`test/fixtures/lane_project/_build/`) — build artifacts, not part of the commit. No impact.

title: DISCOVERY: Literal mutator does not match parsed AST (token_metadata wraps literals)
